ucs.title: [LLM] Stealth Falcon BITS-job C2 / exfiltration via PowerShell or bitsadmin.exe
ucs.description: Hunts Stealth Falcon's signature backdoor (documented by ESET as Win32/StealthFalcon) which abuses the Background Intelligent Transfer Service to stage covert HTTPS C2 and exfiltrate stolen data — either via direct bitsadmin.exe /transfer invocations or via PowerShell loading BITS COM classes (Start-BitsTransfer / BackgroundCopyManager). This is the actor's tradecraft fingerprint, not generic LOLBin abuse.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name="bitsadmin.exe" AND (Processes.process="*/transfer*" OR Processes.process="*/addfile*" OR Processes.process="*/setnotifycmdline*" OR Processes.process="*/setminretrydelay*" OR Processes.process="*/setcustomheaders*" OR Processes.process="*http*")) OR (Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") AND (Processes.process="*Start-BitsTransfer*" OR Processes.process="*BackgroundCopyManager*" OR Processes.process="*BITSAdmin*" OR Processes.process="*Microsoft.BackgroundIntelligentTransfer*")) by host Processes.process_name Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name _time span=1m | where user!="SYSTEM" AND parent!="WindowsUpdateBox.exe" AND parent!="TiWorker.exe" AND parent!="MoUsoCoreWorker.exe" | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner host [| tstats summariesonly=t values(All_Traffic.dest) as dest values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as dest_port from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.app="bitsadmin.exe" OR All_Traffic.app="svchost.exe" by host _time span=5m] | where firstTime <= _time AND _time <= lastTime+300 | sort -firstTime
ucs.kql:
// Stealth Falcon — BITS-based C2 (ESET Win32/StealthFalcon, 2019)
let lookback = 7d;
let ms_signed_parents = dynamic(["WindowsUpdateBox.exe","TiWorker.exe","MoUsoCoreWorker.exe","sdiagnhost.exe","OneDriveSetup.exe"]);
let bits_proc = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"   // exclude machine accounts
    | where (FileName =~ "bitsadmin.exe"
             and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("/transfer","/addfile","/setnotifycmdline","/setcustomheaders","/setminretrydelay"))
         or (FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
             and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("Start-BitsTransfer","BackgroundCopyManager","Microsoft.BackgroundIntelligentTransfer","BITSAdmin"))
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (ms_signed_parents)
    | project ProcTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              FileName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName,
              InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
// Correlate to a same-host outbound HTTPS connection from BITS host process within 10 min
bits_proc
| join kind=inner (
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
    | where RemoteIPType == "Public"
    | where RemotePort in (80, 443, 8080, 8443)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("svchost.exe","bitsadmin.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
    | project NetTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, RemoteIP, RemoteUrl, RemotePort,
              NetProc = InitiatingProcessFileName, NetCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine
  ) on DeviceId
| where NetTime between (ProcTime .. ProcTime + 10m)
| summarize Connections = count(),
            DistinctRemotes = dcount(RemoteIP),
            SampleUrls = make_set(RemoteUrl, 8),
            SampleIPs  = make_set(RemoteIP, 8)
            by DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName, ProcTime
| where DistinctRemotes >= 1
| order by ProcTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Stealth Falcon — BITS-based C2 (Win32/StealthFalcon)
let lookback = 7d;
let ms_signed_parents = dynamic(["WindowsUpdateBox.exe","TiWorker.exe","MoUsoCoreWorker.exe","sdiagnhost.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where (NewProcessName endswith "\\bitsadmin.exe"
         and CommandLine has_any ("/transfer","/addfile","/setnotifycmdline","/setcustomheaders","/setminretrydelay"))
     or ((NewProcessName endswith "\\powershell.exe" or NewProcessName endswith "\\pwsh.exe")
         and CommandLine has_any ("Start-BitsTransfer","BackgroundCopyManager","Microsoft.BackgroundIntelligentTransfer","BITSAdmin"))
| extend ParentLeaf = tostring(split(ParentProcessName, "\\")[-1])
| where ParentLeaf !in~ (ms_signed_parents)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          ParentProcessName, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Stealth Falcon BITS Job Abuse for Covert C2
id: 4f7c2a18-8b65-4c3a-9d11-bits-stealthfalcon
status: experimental
description: Detects Stealth Falcon backdoor C2/exfil tradecraft via bitsadmin.exe /transfer or PowerShell BITS COM class invocation
references:
  - https://www.welivesecurity.com/2019/09/09/backdoor-stealth-falcon-group/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0038/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.command_and_control
  - attack.t1071.001
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.g0038
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_bitsadmin:
    Image|endswith: '\bitsadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '/transfer'
      - '/addfile'
      - '/setnotifycmdline'
      - '/setcustomheaders'
      - '/setminretrydelay'
  selection_pwsh_bits:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'Start-BitsTransfer'
      - 'BackgroundCopyManager'
      - 'Microsoft.BackgroundIntelligentTransfer'
  filter_legit:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\WindowsUpdateBox.exe'
      - '\TiWorker.exe'
      - '\MoUsoCoreWorker.exe'
      - '\sdiagnhost.exe'
  condition: (selection_bitsadmin or selection_pwsh_bits) and not filter_legit
falsepositives:
  - Software-distribution agents using BITS for content delivery
  - Legitimate Windows Update / SCCM workflows
level: high
ucs.rationale: Stealth Falcon's backdoor (G0038, ESET 2019 'Win32/StealthFalcon') is unusual among APTs in that it deliberately uses the BITS service for C2 staging and exfiltration — most APT C2 uses raw sockets or wininet. Pairing the BITS-job artefact (bitsadmin.exe /transfer flags or PowerShell BITS COM classes) with an outbound HTTPS connection from the same host within 10 minutes captures this exact tradec
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] Stealth Falcon scheduled-task PowerShell collecting Windows Vault & browser credential stores
ucs.description: Hunts Stealth Falcon's post-compromise credential harvesting pattern: a scheduled task (T1053.005) launches PowerShell (T1059.001) which then reads the Windows Credential Manager Vault directory or Chromium 'Login Data' SQLite database (T1555.003/T1555.004) and stages output for exfil. This combined sequence is characteristic of the actor's spyware against UAE journalists/dissidents.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as ps_cmd values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") AND Processes.parent_process_name IN ("svchost.exe","taskeng.exe","schtasks.exe","WmiPrvSE.exe") by host Processes.process_id _time span=1m | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner host process_id [| tstats summariesonly=t values(Filesystem.file_path) as touched_files from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_path="*\\Microsoft\\Vault\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Microsoft\\Credentials\\*" OR Filesystem.file_name="Login Data" OR Filesystem.file_name="Cookies" OR Filesystem.file_name="Web Data" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Mozilla\\Firefox\\Profiles\\*key*.db*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Mozilla\\Firefox\\Profiles\\*logins.json*") by host Filesystem.process_id] | where user!="SYSTEM" | sort -firstTime
ucs.kql:
// Stealth Falcon — scheduled-task PowerShell harvesting Vault + browser creds
let lookback = 7d;
let sched_parents = dynamic(["svchost.exe","taskeng.exe","schtasks.exe","WmiPrvSE.exe"]);
let cred_paths = dynamic([
    @"\Microsoft\Vault\",
    @"\Microsoft\Credentials\",
    @"\Microsoft\Protect\",
    @"\Google\Chrome\User Data\",
    @"\Microsoft\Edge\User Data\",
    @"\Mozilla\Firefox\Profiles\",
    @"\BraveSoftware\Brave-Browser\User Data\"
]);
let cred_files = dynamic(["Login Data","Cookies","Web Data","logins.json","key4.db","key3.db","Local State"]);
// 1. PowerShell launched by a scheduled-task / WMI host
let ps_via_sched = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (sched_parents)
         or (InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "svchost.exe"
             and InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any ("Schedule","netsvcs"))
    | project ProcTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              PsId = ProcessId, PsCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
// 2. Same PowerShell PID then touches a credential store
ps_via_sched
| join kind=inner (
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
    | where FolderPath has_any (cred_paths) or FileName in~ (cred_files)
    | project FileTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, InitiatingProcessId,
              FolderPath, FileName, ActionType
  ) on DeviceId, $left.PsId == $right.InitiatingProcessId
| where FileTime between (ProcTime .. ProcTime + 30m)
| summarize CredArtefacts = make_set(strcat(FolderPath, FileName), 25),
            ArtefactCount = dcount(strcat(FolderPath, FileName)),
            FirstAccess = min(FileTime), LastAccess = max(FileTime)
            by DeviceName, AccountName, Parent, ParentCmd, PsCmd, ProcTime
| where ArtefactCount >= 2   // single-file touches are noisy; chained access is the signal
| order by ProcTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Stealth Falcon — scheduled-task PowerShell + credential-store access
let lookback = 7d;
let sched_parents = dynamic(["\\svchost.exe","\\taskeng.exe","\\schtasks.exe","\\WmiPrvSE.exe"]);
let cred_paths = dynamic([
    @"\Microsoft\Vault\",
    @"\Microsoft\Credentials\",
    @"\Google\Chrome\User Data\",
    @"\Microsoft\Edge\User Data\",
    @"\Mozilla\Firefox\Profiles\"
]);
let cred_files = dynamic(["Login Data","Cookies","Web Data","logins.json","key4.db","Local State"]);
let ps_via_sched = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | where NewProcessName endswith "\\powershell.exe" or NewProcessName endswith "\\pwsh.exe"
    | where ParentProcessName has_any (sched_parents)
    | project ProcTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              PsPid = NewProcessId, PsCmd = CommandLine,
              Parent = ParentProcessName;
ps_via_sched
| join kind=inner (
    ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
    | where ActingProcessName endswith "powershell.exe" or ActingProcessName endswith "pwsh.exe"
    | where TargetFilePath has_any (cred_paths) or TargetFileName in~ (cred_files)
    | project FileTime = TimeGenerated, Computer = DvcHostname,
              ActingProcessId, TargetFilePath, TargetFileName, EventType
  ) on Computer
| where FileTime between (ProcTime .. ProcTime + 30m)
| summarize CredArtefacts = make_set(strcat(TargetFilePath, TargetFileName), 25),
            ArtefactCount = dcount(strcat(TargetFilePath, TargetFileName)),
            FirstAccess = min(FileTime), LastAccess = max(FileTime)
            by Computer, Account, Parent, PsCmd, ProcTime
| where ArtefactCount >= 2
| order by ProcTime desc
ucs.rationale: Stealth Falcon's targeted spyware against Emirati journalists/activists relies on persistent scheduled tasks (T1053.005) that drop a PowerShell-based collector (T1059.001) which then loots both the Windows Credential Vault (T1555.004) and browser-saved credentials (T1555.003). Catching scheduled-task-spawned PowerShell in isolation is noisy; chaining it via PID/host to file access against ≥2 disti
